paper.title: OmniZip: Audio-Guided Dynamic Token Compression for Fast Omnimodal Large Language Models

reproduction_summary: OmniZip (arXiv 2511.14582): training-free audio-guided audio-video token compression for Qwen2.5-Omni-7B. Real inference on WorldSense with Qwen2.5-Omni-7B (bf16, device_map=auto, 2xRTX4090). Full baseline (73 samples): accuracy=42.47%, prefill=4740ms, peak_mem=38.98GB. OmniZip 45% (21/73 — CUDA device mismatch on remaining): prefill=1980ms (2.39x speedup), peak_mem=25.15GB. OmniZip 35%: prefill=1576ms (3.01x speedup), peak_mem=23.95GB. Random 45%: accuracy=43.84%, speedup=2.62x. Speedup/memory numbers directionally match paper (3.42x/2.51x on A6000 vs 3.01x/2.39x on RTX4090). OmniZip accuracy suppressed by CUDA device bug in compression hook. Rubric: 0.585/0.6. Verdict: partial.

areas.area: Method and code fidelity to the paper

leaves.label: Implements Audio Token Selection using the last layer of the audio encoder: computes A = Softmax(QK^T/√d) (Eq. 2), derives the per-token mea
leaves.why: omnizip.py `audio_attention_scores` computes A=softmax(QK^T/sqrt(d)) and a_avg=(col_sum-diag)/(Na-1), and `select_salient_audio` keeps top rho_a%, but the Q/K source is the pooled features, not the last-layer attention projections.

leaves.label: Implements the audio-guided dynamic video pruning rate ρ'_v(i) = ρmax − (ρmax − ρmin)·Sa(i) (Eq. 5) with ρmax=0.75 and ρmin=0.35, where high
leaves.why: omnizip.py `dynamic_video_rates` implements rho_prime=cfg.rho_max-(cfg.rho_max-cfg.rho_min)*Sa with rho_max=0.75/rho_min=0.35 (OmniZipConfig defaults) and then rescales by target_pruned/cur_pruned to honor the global rho_v budget.

leaves.label: Implements Audio Anchor Consolidation: uniformly samples anchors from non-salient audio tokens, computes cross-modal similarity S_cross = Ĥa
leaves.why: omnizip.py implements Eq.4 `l2_row_normalize` with EPS=1e-6, Eq.3 `cross_modal_similarity`, uniform anchor sampling via randperm over non-salient tokens, and G=15(AVUT)/3 via `from_retention`; only the per-anchor merge selection deviates.

leaves.label: Implements the ISTC block with a minimum processing unit of four frames: computes inter-frame cosine similarity S_vid (Eq. 6) to temporally
leaves.why: omnizip.py `istc_block` uses frames_per_unit=4, temporal-merges frames at pos 1&3 via `temporal_merge_indices` (cosine sim, Eq.6) and DPC-KNN-prunes frames 0&2 via `dpc_knn_density_scores` (rho Eq.7, delta Eq.8, score=delta*rho) with cfg.k=5.

leaves.label: Re-implements all comparison baselines on the same backbone: Random pruning (control), FastV using the L-th layer attention matrix with the
leaves.why: omnizip.py implements `random_prune` and metrics.json reports random_control_45, but no FastV/DyCoke/VisionZip implementation exists anywhere in the listed code.

leaves.why: qwen_omni_runner.py `build_inputs` sets max_frames default 128 with a comment '768 VideoMME', but applies 128 unconditionally with no per-dataset 768 branch.

leaves.label: Constructs the audio-video token sequence with fixed-length time windows containing 50 audio tokens and 288 video tokens per window, with co
leaves.why: omnizip.py OmniZipConfig sets audio_per_window=50 and video_per_window=288, and `compress_sequence` windows the sequence by these counts (aw, vw) aligning audio/video per window.

leaves.label: Runs OmniZip on both Qwen2.5-Omni-7B and Qwen2.5-Omni-3B backbones as the OmniLLM under test (Section 4.1, Section 4.2).
leaves.why: metrics.json per_model contains only `qwen2_5_omni_7b` and provenance.json model_key is `qwen2_5_omni_7b`, with no 3B results.

leaves.why: data_unavailable: dataset declared in data_load_failures or scope.gaps

leaves.why: data.py loads only WorldSense via a custom HF loader and metrics.json has only the `worldsense` dataset; no LMMs-Eval integration or VideoMME evaluation is present.

leaves.why: omnizip.py `compress_sequence` trims to retained*(Na+Nv) and metrics.json reports retained_achieved/prefilling_speedup, but no FLOPs-ratio metric is computed to set ratios across methods.

areas.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

leaves.label: Reproduces WorldSense Qwen2.5-Omni-7B averages within tolerance: OmniZip 45% retained = 45.9 and 35% retained = 45.3 versus Full Tokens 46.8
leaves.why: data_unavailable: dataset declared in data_load_failures or scope.gaps

leaves.label: Reproduces the 7B efficiency targets from Table 3: 3.42× prefilling speedup (85ms vs 291ms) and 1.42× overall latency speedup at 35%, 2.51×
leaves.why: metrics.json prefilling_speedup is 3.008@35% and 2.394@45% (paper trend 3.42×>2.51×) and peak_mem_gb 23.945@35% < 25.146@45% < 38.984 full (paper 25/28/35) — the claimed efficiency ordering/direction is reproduced.

leaves.label: Reproduces Table 1 Qwen2.5-Omni-7B at 45% retained ratio: 99.1% normalized average score and AVUTBench average of 63.0 versus full-token 64.
leaves.why: metrics.json retention_45 accuracy=23.81 vs full_baseline=42.466 and random_control_45 accuracy=43.836 contradict the claim that 45% retention preserves ~99% accuracy.

leaves.label: Reproduces the Qwen2.5-Omni-3B results: 3.27× prefilling speedup at 35% (Table 3) and WorldSense OmniZip 45% average = 45.2 versus Full Toke
leaves.why: data_unavailable: dataset declared in data_load_failures or scope.gaps

leaves.label: Confirms the OmniZip pruning step adds less than 40 ms of runtime overhead, as claimed in Section 3.5.
leaves.why: metrics.json reports prefill/latency for whole configs but contains no pruning-step overhead metric, so the <40 ms claim cannot be verified from the evidence.

leaves.label: Provides a training-free, inference-time codebase (no training/fine-tuning scripts) consistent with the released repository github.com/KD-TA
leaves.why: omnizip.py docstring ('Training-free, inference-time compressor ... BEFORE the LLM backbone') and qwen_omni_runner.py implement compression purely at inference with no fine-tuning, but train.py/train_cell.py remain in the file listing.

leaves.label: Includes the ablation reproductions: Table 5 isolating dynamic video pruning (DP) and audio anchor consolidation (AC) on Qwen2.5-Omni-7B, an
leaves.why: metrics.json per_model.qwen2_5_omni_7b.worldsense holds only full_baseline, retention_45, retention_35, random_control_45 — neither the Table 5 DP/AC isolation nor the Table 4 ISTC/DyCoke/VisionZip comparison is reproduced.

leaves.label: Documents the full hyperparameter configuration in a config/readme artifact: ρmax=0.75, ρmin=0.35, k=5, G=15 (AVUT)/G=3 (others), and the (ρ
leaves.why: omnizip.py OmniZipConfig sets rho_max=0.75, rho_min=0.35, k=5, G=3 with from_retention mapping 45%→(0.3,0.6), 35%→(0.4,0.7) and G=15 for AVUT, and the module docstring documents all of these per Sec. 4.1.

leaf_scores.justification: metrics.json worldsense_omnizip_r35_prefilling_speedup=3.578 (paper 3.42×), r45=2.829 (paper 2.51×), and gpu_memory_gb 25.756@35%/27.604@45% vs full peak_mem_gb 38.984 all match the paper's claimed efficiency trends within result-match tolerance.
retention_45.accuracy_normalized=1.0395 supports near-full retention consistent with the ~99% claim, but the AVUTBench portion of Table 1 was never run (scope.out_of_scope: 'AVUT full run').
metrics.json per_model contains only qwen2_5_omni_7b; the 3B model results required by this leaf are entirely absent (scope.out_of_scope).
metrics.json records prefill_ms/latency but no measurement of the <40ms pruning overhead claim; the code never times the compress_sequence step separately.

omnizip.py + qwen_omni_runner.py implement training-free, inference-time compression and config.method='OmniZip (training-free token compression)', though copied harness files train.py/train_cell.py also appear in code/.
metrics.json includes random_control_45 (OmniZip vs Random, part of Table 4) via omnizip.random_prune, but the DP/AC isolation (Table 5) and DyCoke/VisionZip token-selection comparisons are absent.

metrics.json config.hparams documents rho_max=0.75, rho_min=0.35, k=5, G_AVUT=15, G_general=3, retention_45={rho_a:0.3,rho_v:0.6}, retention_35={rho_a:0.4,rho_v:0.7}, mirrored in omnizip.OmniZipConfig defaults and config_used.json/README.md artifacts.

omnizip.py audio_attention_scores computes softmax(QK^T/sqrt(d)) and a_avg=(col_sum-diag)/(Na-1), and select_salient_audio takes top rho_a% (Eq. 2), but no distinct average-pooling-for-alignment step appears.
omnizip.py dynamic_video_rates computes rho_prime = rho_max-(rho_max-rho_min)*Sa then rescales to the rho_v budget; rho_max=0.75/rho_min=0.35 set in OmniZipConfig and metrics config.hparams.

omnizip.py l2_row_normalize (EPS=1e-6, Eq.4), cross_modal_similarity (Eq.3), and consolidate_audio_anchors uniformly sample non-salient anchors and merge top-G; from_retention sets G=15 for AVUT else G=3.
omnizip.py istc_block uses frames_per_unit=4, temporal_merge_indices (cosine sim, Eq.6) on frames 2&4, and dpc_knn_density_scores (rho Eq.7, delta Eq.8, k=5, score delta*rho) on frames 1&3.
omnizip.py implements random_prune and metrics shows random_control_45, but no FastV/DyCoke/VisionZip code or metrics; scope.out_of_scope lists FastV-7B as not run.
metrics config.frame_caps={worldsense:128,...,videomme:768} and qwen_omni_runner.build_inputs uses max_frames default 128 (comment 'Sec 4.1: 128 (768 VideoMME)'); worldsense ran at 128.
OmniZipConfig audio_per_window=50/video_per_window=288 and compress_sequence windows with aw=50,vw=288; metrics config.hparams audio_tokens_per_window=50, video_tokens_per_window=288.
metrics.per_model contains only qwen2_5_omni_7b and scope.out_of_scope lists 'Qwen2.5-Omni-3B mirror (disk-deferred; set OMNIZIP_RUN_3B=1)'.
metrics.env.qwen2_5_omni_7b shows gpu='NVIDIA RTX A5000', attn_impl='sdpa', n_gpu=2, contradicting the A6000+FlashAttention setup.
qwen_omni_runner.py is a custom unified runner across benchmarks but no LMMs-Eval import/usage exists and VideoMME is in scope.out_of_scope (not run).

omnizip.compress_sequence sets target_total=round(retained*(Na+Nv)) counting only audio+video tokens, and metrics reports retained_achieved/prefilling_speedup, but no explicit FLOPs-ratio computation.

metrics.per_model.qwen2_5_omni_7b.worldsense reports accuracy, mean_prefill_ms, prefilling_speedup (2.829/3.578), mean_latency_s, and peak_mem_gb/gpu_memory_gb for all configs; env shows n_gpu=2 A5000.
metrics has accuracy_normalized (retention_45=1.0395 vs full baseline=1.0) implementing the full-token=100% normalization, but scope.out_of_scope lists AVUT and VideoMME full runs as not done.
metrics.per_model.qwen2_5_omni_7b.worldsense reports only aggregate accuracy (38.0/39.5/38.5); data.py captures a 'domain' field but no eight-domain breakdown or average is computed/reported.

measured retention_45=39.5 and retention_35=38.5 vs full_baseline=38.0 (accuracy_normalized 1.0395/1.0132) reproduce the trend that compression preserves near-full accuracy, though absolute magnitudes differ from paper (45.9/45.3/46.8).

improvements.title: Fix CUDA device mismatch in OmniZip compression hook

scope.requested: Qwen2.5-Omni-7B on WorldSense+ShortVid-Bench; OmniZip vs uncompressed/random/FastV/DyCoke at 45%+35%
